question: Across three deterministic lattice physics families, which family, parameter profile, and spatial dimension combinations occupy persistent active-and-spatially-structured regimes under a fixed degree and approximately fixed cell budget?
experimental_unit: one independently seeded periodic lattice world

hypotheses.prediction: Literature-informed binary, Gray-Scott, and cyclic profiles have higher active_structured_candidate occupancy than their static, global-churn, and mechanism-limit controls on confirmatory and holdout seeds.
hypotheses.falsifier: Candidate occupancy is not enriched over controls, or a static/global-churn control passes the same frozen conjunction at a comparable rate.

hypotheses.prediction: The empirical regime distribution depends detectably on the physics family; no single entropy or turnover threshold identifies the candidate regime across all families.
hypotheses.falsifier: Family-conditioned regime occupancies are effectively indistinguishable and either entropy or turnover alone separates every candidate from every control.

hypotheses.prediction: With neighborhood degree fixed at 12 and cell count approximately matched, profile/family differences in candidate occupancy exceed within-profile differences across dimensions 2-4.
hypotheses.falsifier: The dimension-associated occupancy range is at least as large as the profile/family-associated range on both confirmatory and holdout splits.

hypotheses.prediction: Static controls retain state diversity while global-churn controls retain high entropy and turnover, but both fail the multi-axis candidate conjunction.
hypotheses.falsifier: A static or global-churn control passes the candidate conjunction, or the audit cannot exhibit a high individual diagnostic on either negative-control class.

model.update_order: Synchronous periodic-lattice updates. Binary and cyclic rules consume the complete pre-update state. Gray-Scott uses explicit Euler substeps with a degree-normalized discrete Laplacian.
model.rng: numpy.default_rng(seed) initializes each world. The baseline and single-site perturbation share the same initialized state; all later updates are deterministic and draw no random numbers.

literature_priors.design_use: finite-amplitude central perturbation, periodic domain, Du/Dv=2 profile, and equal-diffusion/reaction-off mechanism limits
successor-color contact thresholds and synchronous cyclic updates
degree-matched-12 offsets and fraction-scaled versus literal B3/S23 profiles

seed_plan.pairing: The same numeric seed and family-specific initializer pair dimensions and profiles where shapes permit; profiles within a family receive exactly the same initialized baseline and perturbation location for a given split, dimension, and seed.

denominators.turnover: all lattice sites between consecutive recorded states
denominators.mutual_information: all directed degree-matched neighbor pairs or equally many pairs from a frozen family of deterministic affine permutations
denominators.perturbation_response: differing sites divided by all lattice sites; gain is relative to the one-site initial perturbation
denominators.regime_occupancy: independently seeded episodes in the declared condition and split

exposure.eligibility: Every site is eligible for exactly one synchronous rule evaluation per solver step under the declared family/profile.
exposure.activation: Every non-control update evaluates its reaction, birth/survival, or successor-contact rule from the complete pre-update state.
exposure.state_change: Changed-site counts and categorical turnover are retained per recorded tick for both baseline and paired perturbation trajectories.

analysis.uncertainty: Condition occupancy uses episode-level Jeffreys Beta(1/2,1/2) posterior intervals. Metric summaries use deterministic episode-level bootstrap intervals; ticks and cells are repeated measurements, never replicates.
analysis.multiple_comparisons: All 36 declared family/profile/dimension cells and every negative control are reported. No champion is selected and no cell-specific threshold is fitted.
analysis.stopping_rule: Run every declared episode unless wall time exceeds 1200 seconds, process RSS exceeds 2048 MB, retained artifacts exceed 250 MB, or deterministic replay fails. Profiles were frozen before discovery; the one logged classifier correction is frozen before all confirmatory and holdout execution.

discovery_calibration.policy: Discovery rows may expose a classifier defect, but any correction must be logged here and frozen before confirmatory or holdout execution; no confirmatory or holdout row may be inspected first.
discovery_calibration.change: Raised entropy_max_inclusive from 0.98 to the normalization ceiling 1.0. The discovery cyclic q=6 threshold=2 arm had strong excess neighbor mutual information but was rejected solely by near-maximal marginal color entropy. A pre-confirmation unit test also showed that a half-torus far-shift null aliases periodic stripes, so the excess-MI baseline was replaced by an equal-sized frozen family of deterministic affine-permutation pairs. The local-neighbor statistic, profiles, seeds, lower thresholds, turnover bound, and simulator dynamics did not change.
discovery_calibration.freeze_point: after discovery and before valid confirmatory/holdout execution
discovery_calibration.invalid_execution_note: Two production attempts were invalidated without inspecting confirmatory outcomes because a Gray-Scott center perturbation for one seed did not cross a categorical bin. The paired Gray perturbation was therefore standardized to move center V to the center of its adjacent 1/8 bin, guaranteeing exactly one initially differing site. Both partial raw files were overwritten from episode zero; no partial row is accepted as evidence.

budget.execution: Sequential local CPU execution; no worker parallelism, shared RNG, GPU, or external model call.

paired_perturbations.cyclic_ca: advance the center by one color
paired_perturbations.gray_scott: move center V to the center of the adjacent 1/8 concentration bin
